# Executive Summary

`igvShiny` embeds the Integrative Genomics Viewer as an htmlwidget in R Shiny, and is one of only two Bioconductor packages bridging IGV to the R ecosystem. With 228 monthly downloads and 27 forks, it has a real user base — but the package currently has **1 FAIL and 7 NOTEs** on Bioconductor, nine open issues (some since 2022), and no automated tests. The original author retired; I took over maintenance and have been shipping fixes, but a real cleanup pass needs focused time I haven't had.

This proposal requests **$10,000** to do that cleanup properly, and to add something no Bioconductor package currently has: a natural language interface to a genome browser.

Five milestones over 9 months: fix the bugs, complete the API, build a real test suite, improve documentation, and ship `igvChatUI()` / `igvChatServer()` — an optional module that lets users navigate the browser and manage tracks by typing prompts, powered by `elmer` (Claude, OpenAI, or a local Ollama model). The local LLM option matters for hospitals where sending patient genomic data to an external API is simply not allowed.
